title: INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, Upsert, and Mutation Evidence · Query Trace: Upsert Conflict
summary: A ticket import and reassignment workflow must change only intended rows and retain evidence of what happened. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql upsert conflict increment in a guarded ticket-mutation workflow. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use a named uniqueness conflict and explicit update policy for idempotent ingestion.
Distinguish row deletion, truncation, and retention-safe archival while predicting referential effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Predict Upsert Conflict · Theory Upsert Conflict
steps.instruction:
theory case — Import ticket 203 twice, reassign one unowned ticket, reject an invalid severity, and migrate a required category field without breaking the old writer. Preview mutation scope and retain rollback plus changed-row evidence.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Upsert Conflict contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Upsert Conflict · Theory Upsert Conflict.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use a named uniqueness conflict and explicit update policy for idempotent ingestion. For Predict Upsert Conflict · Theory Upsert Conflict, keep Upsert Conflict connected to executable relational evidence.

content.text: Use a named uniqueness conflict and explicit update policy for idempotent ingestion. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Declare result or mutation grain, keys, NULL meaning, and transaction boundary; predict cardinality and invariants; run the smallest query; reconcile counts and plans; then test changed data and dialect limits.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Import ticket 203 twice, reassign one unowned ticket, reject an invalid severity, and migrate a required category field without breaking the old writer. Preview mutation scope and retain rollback plus changed-row evidence. A defensible solution applies Upsert Conflict, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a guarded ticket-mutation workflow.

content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.text: Upsert means overwrite every existing column. This fails the Upsert Conflict contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Upsert Conflict evidence · Theory Upsert Conflict
